original_abstract: This research investigates how discipline and religious values synergize to foster resilience and responsibility among students at SMP Nidaul Quran in Sarolangun Regency. Adopting a qualitative case study design, the study gathered data through in-depth interviews, field observations, and thematic document analysis. The findings reveal that discipline is a fundamental pillar of character formation, particularly when integrated into a pedagogic framework centered on moral development. By successfully aligning disciplinary standards with core religious tenets, SMP Nidaul Quran cultivates a student body characterized by psychological fortitude and accountability. Key drivers of this success include the role of teachers as moral exemplars and the proactive involvement of parents. Ultimately, this research underscores that the integration of religious values transcends academic achievement, instilling a deep sense of integrity. This holistic collaboration between educators, families, and spiritual values offers a robust model for other Islamic institutions, demonstrating that education is most effective when it shapes the heart and soul, preparing students to navigate life’s challenges with steadfastness.
humanized_abstract: The aim of this research is to examine the synergy of disciplinary and religious values and their role in enhancing students’ resilience and sense of responsibility at SMP Nidaul Quran in Sarolangun Regency. This qualitative case study applied the methodology of interview, observation, and thematic analysis. The findings indicate that discipline is one of the most crucial elements of nurturing the characters of students, when taught in conjunction with moral education. Therefore, SMP Nidaul Quran succeeds in instilling discipline upon its students to the extent that they are equipped with stronger psychological resilience and also maximally accountable to ensure that they embody the Islamic values. Variables which contributed towards this success were found to be teachers’ leadership and high involvement of parents. The study demonstrated that religious values extended beyond cognitive skills and had a positive impact upon the students’ sense of obligation in terms of enhancing their personal integrity, thereby confirming the power of successful interaction between teachers, parents and religious values that can serve as alternative learning model for other madrasas to reach the promised goal of educating students for life.
